05_Infection_niche_detection

Source: vignettes/05_Infection_niche_detection.Rmd
05_Infection_niche_detection.Rmd

Overview

Following infection-positive spot identification, the next step is to delineate spatially coherent infection niches. Rather than treating spots independently, ISTtools reconstructs infection regions by grouping neighboring positive spots into continuous spatial domains.

This step transforms discrete detection results into biologically interpretable infection niches, enabling region-level analysis.

Setup 

suppressMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(ISTools)
})

Niche detection (CE)

Spatial niches are identified using region-based clustering. By default, ISTtools supports geometric reconstruction (e.g., convex hull) to define spatial domains.

IST_obj_detect <- NicheDetect_STS(
  IST_obj = IST_obj_after,
  meta_key = "M1_SpotDetect_Gene_TB_correct_after_all_gene_white",
  loop_id = "PI8W_1",
  method_level = "region",
  method_region = "convex",
  update_spots = FALSE,
  k_kNNdist = 4,
  pos_colnm = "Label_all_gene_nFeature(sum)",
  grp_nm = "TB_DBSCAN",
  dir_nm = "M2_NicheDetect_STS"
)

This procedure aggregates spatially proximal infection-positive spots into contiguous regions.

Niche detection (JEV)

The same framework is applicable to viral datasets.

IST_obj_detect <- NicheDetect_STS(
  IST_obj = IST_obj,
  meta_key = "M1_SpotDetect_Gene_JEV_correct_before_all_gene_white",
  method_level = "region",
  method_region = "convex",
  update_spots = FALSE,
  pos_colnm = "Label_all_gene_nFeature(sum)",
  grp_nm = "STS_JEV_microbe_region",
  dir_nm = "M2_NicheDetect_STS"
)

Visualization 

detect_meta <- GetMetaData(
  IST_obj_detect,
  meta_key = "M2_NicheDetect_STS_STS_JEV_microbe_region"
)[[1]]

Plot_Spatial(
  detect_meta,
  "x","y","ROI_region",
  datatype = "discrete"
)

Remarks

  • Region construction imposes spatial continuity on infection signals
  • The choice of method_region influences niche boundary geometry
  • Output niches serve as the basis for downstream spatial analyses

Session information 

sessionInfo()

On this page
